meanings.definition: location near or direction toward the left side; i.e. the side to the north when a person or object faces east
examples: she stood on the left

meanings.definition: those who support varying degrees of social or political or economic change designed to promote the public welfare
the hand that is on the left side of the body
examples: jab with your left

meanings.definition: the piece of ground in the outfield on the catcher's left
examples: the batter flied out to left

meanings.definition: a turn toward the side of the body that is on the north when the person is facing east
examples: take a left at the corner

meanings.definition: go away from a place
examples: At what time does your train leave?
She didn't leave until midnight
The ship leaves at midnight

meanings.definition: go and leave behind, either intentionally or by neglect or forgetfulness
examples: She left a mess when she moved out
His good luck finally left him
her husband left her after 20 years of marriage
she wept thinking she had been left behind

meanings.definition: act or be so as to become in a specified state
examples: The inflation left them penniless
The president's remarks left us speechless

meanings.definition: leave unchanged or undisturbed or refrain from taking
examples: leave it as is
leave the young fawn alone
leave the flowers that you see in the park behind

meanings.definition: move out of or depart from
examples: leave the room
the fugitive has left the country

meanings.definition: make a possibility or provide opportunity for; permit to be attainable or cause to remain
examples: This leaves no room for improvement
The evidence allows only one conclusion
allow for mistakes
leave lots of time for the trip
This procedure provides for lots of leeway

meanings.definition: have as a result or residue
examples: The water left a mark on the silk dress
Her blood left a stain on the napkin

meanings.definition: remove oneself from an association with or participation in
examples: She wants to leave
The teenager left home
She left her position with the Red Cross
He left the Senate after two terms
after 20 years with the same company, she pulled up stakes

meanings.definition: put into the care or protection of someone
examples: He left the decision to his deputy
leave your child the nurse's care

meanings.definition: leave or give by will after one's death
examples: My aunt bequeathed me all her jewelry
My grandfather left me his entire estate

meanings.definition: have left or have as a remainder
examples: That left the four of us
19 minus 8 leaves 11

meanings.definition: be survived by after one's death
examples: He left six children
At her death, she left behind her husband and 11 cats

meanings.definition: transmit (knowledge or skills)
examples: give a secret to the Russians
leave your name and address here
impart a new skill to the students

meanings.definition: leave behind unintentionally
examples: I forgot my umbrella in the restaurant
I left my keys inside the car and locked the doors

meanings.definition: being or located on or directed toward the side of the body to the west when facing north
examples: my left hand
left center field
the left bank of a river is bank on your left side when you are facing downstream

meanings.definition: not used up
examples: leftover meatloaf
she had a little money left over so she went to a movie
some odd dollars left
saved the remaining sandwiches for supper
unexpended provisions

meanings.definition: intended for the left hand
examples: I rarely lose a left-hand glove

meanings.definition: of or belonging to the political or intellectual left
toward or on the left; also used figuratively
examples: he looked right and left
the political party has moved left
